title: euploca collina
intro: Euploca collina, also known as "Tecapán", is a shrubby plant species native to Central America and Mexico, belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its leaves, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Euploca collina include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Euploca collina include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its full range of medicinal uses.
